g.data-narration: Welcome! In this lecture, we will explore how to measure cache efficiency in microcontroller systems for optimal performance.
g.text: Understanding Cache Performance Metrics in Microcontrollers

g.data-narration: Cache memory is a high-speed storage between CPU and main memory, storing frequently accessed data to improve access speed.
A cache HIT means data is found in cache (fast access), while a MISS requires fetching from main memory (slower).
The cache hit ratio equals hits divided by total accesses. Miss rate is simply one minus the hit ratio.
g.text: Cache Hit Ratio and Miss Rate

g.data-narration: In this example, 8500 hits out of 10000 accesses gives an 85% hit ratio and 15% miss rate.
Average Memory Access Time combines hit time with miss penalty. AMAT equals hit time plus miss rate multiplied by miss penalty.
g.text: Miss Penalty = Main Memory Access - Cache Access Time

g.data-narration: With 2 cycle hit time, 8% miss rate, and 23 cycle penalty, AMAT becomes 3.84 cycles - a 6.51x speedup!
g.text: Hit Time: 2 cycles | Miss Rate: 8% | Main Memory: 25 cycles

g.data-narration: Cache efficiency measures performance improvement: the percentage reduction in access time compared to having no cache.
After optimization, AMAT improved from 8 to 4 cycles with 20-cycle main memory, increasing efficiency from 60% to 80%.
The three C's of cache misses are: Compulsory (first access), Capacity (cache too small), and Conflict (mapping collisions).
Four methods measure cache performance: simulation, hardware counters, software instrumentation, and analytical modeling.
Key formulas to remember: hit ratio, AMAT, and cache efficiency. These are essential for analyzing cache performance.
g.text: AMAT = Hit Time + (Miss Rate x Miss Penalty)

g.data-narration: Remember: higher hit ratios mean better performance, AMAT combines all factors, and the three C's help diagnose cache issues.
g.text: Cache efficiency directly impacts microcontroller execution speed and real-time responsiveness
Higher hit ratios (typically greater than 90%) indicate better cache performance
AMAT provides a single metric combining all cache performance factors

g.data-narration: Understanding cache efficiency measurement helps optimize embedded systems for better performance and power efficiency.
g.text: Apply these concepts to measure and optimize cache performance in your embedded systems
